entries.summary: Phase 4 API and Engine Layer Audit complete. Audited api_server.py (666 lines, 8 endpoints), rag_engine.py (524 lines), engine_factory.py (207 lines), main.py (131 lines), and llm_interface.py (503 lines). Produced 53 total findings (0 CRITICAL after validation, 14 HIGH, 26 MEDIUM, 13 LOW) across 3 evidence files. Architect caught 5 false positives: api-004 (/ingest returns generic msg not str(e)), api-015 (37 lines not a god function), llm-001/002 (SSRF overblown for local app), llm-003/004 (RuntimeError prevents None backend), and correctly reframed llm-009 (init-time fallback is correct; issue is no runtime fallback).

metadata.plan_id: Comprehensive Codebase QA Review — AI-Hardened Edition

top_rejection_reasons: false_positive: /ingest returns generic message not str(e)
false_positive: 37 lines not a god function
severity_downgrade: SSRF overstated for local-only app
severity_downgrade: type error has runtime guard (RuntimeError)
lessons_learned: Explorer tends to overestimate severity for security findings on local-only apps — always validate against threat model (localhost CORS, user-controlled config)
Runtime control flow analysis (like RuntimeError preventing None backend) is critical for type safety findings — LSP flags don't tell the full story
False positive rate increased in Phase 4 (5 FP vs 2 in Phase 3) as file complexity grew — larger files produce more speculative findings
Information disclosure via str(e) in HTTP responses is the most common security finding in API code — systematic pattern
Dead streaming code with type errors (llm_interface lines 166-199) is a high-confidence finding — code path never executed but contains real bugs
